annotations.label: Hyperbolic functions (geometric)

anchor.snippet: hyperbolic functions are analogues of the ordinary trigonometric functions

annotations.note: Mathlib defines the hyperbolic family (`Real.sinh`/`cosh`/`tanh` and `Complex` versions) although not via the geometric framing.

annotations.label: Unit hyperbola parametrization by (cosh t, sinh t)

anchor.snippet: the points (cosh t , sinh t ) form the right half of the unit hyperbola

annotations.note: `cosh_sq_sub_sinh_sq : cosh x ^ 2 - sinh x ^ 2 = 1` implies (cosh t, sinh t) lies on the unit hyperbola; the surjection onto the right branch is not packaged as a dedicated Mathlib lemma.

annotations.label: Derivatives of sinh and cosh

anchor.snippet: the derivatives of sinh( t ) and cosh( t ) are cosh( t ) and sinh( t ) respectively

annotations.note: `Real.deriv_sinh : deriv sinh = cosh` and `Real.deriv_cosh : deriv cosh = sinh` are both in DerivHyp.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic sine

anchor.snippet: hyperbolic sine

annotations.note: `Real.sinh` (and `Complex.sinh`) are defined in `Mathlib.Analysis.Complex.Trigonometric`.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic cosine

anchor.snippet: hyperbolic cosine

annotations.note: `Real.cosh` and `Complex.cosh` are defined as `(exp z + exp (-z))/2`.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic tangent

anchor.snippet: hyperbolic tangent

annotations.note: `Real.tanh` and `Complex.tanh` are defined as `sinh/cosh`.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic cotangent

anchor.snippet: hyperbolic cotangent

annotations.note: No `coth`/`Real.coth`/`Complex.coth` declaration is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic secant

anchor.snippet: hyperbolic secant

annotations.note: No `sech` declaration is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic cosecant

anchor.snippet: hyperbolic cosecant

annotations.note: No `csch` declaration is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Inverse hyperbolic functions

anchor.snippet: The inverse hyperbolic functions are

annotations.note: `Real.arsinh`, `Real.arcosh`, `Real.artanh` are defined; the other three inverses (arcoth/arsech/arcsch) are absent.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic angle

anchor.snippet: The magnitude of a hyperbolic angle is the area of its hyperbolic sector

annotations.note: No formalization of hyperbolic angle / hyperbolic sector area in Mathlib.

annotations.label: sinh and cosh are entire

anchor.snippet: The hyperbolic sine and the hyperbolic cosine are entire functions

annotations.note: `Complex.analyticOnNhd_sinh`/`analyticOnNhd_cosh` (and `differentiable_sinh`/`differentiable_cosh`) state that they are entire.

annotations.label: Other hyperbolic functions meromorphic

anchor.snippet: the other hyperbolic functions are meromorphic in the whole complex plane

annotations.note: Only `Complex.meromorphic_tanh` is formalized; coth/sech/csch are not even defined.

annotations.label: Transcendence via Lindemann–Weierstrass

anchor.snippet: the hyperbolic functions have a transcendental value for every non-zero algebraic value of the argument

annotations.note: Only the analytical part of Lindemann–Weierstrass is in Mathlib; the corollary for hyperbolic values is not stated.

annotations.label: Exponential definition (sinh and cosh)

anchor.snippet: the hyperbolic functions sinh and cosh can be defined with the exponential function

annotations.note: `Real.sinh_eq`/`Real.cosh_eq` give the exponential formulas; the Complex versions are the literal definitions.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic sine as odd part of exp

anchor.snippet: Hyperbolic sine: the odd part of the exponential function

annotations.note: The Mathlib definition `Complex.sinh z = (exp z - exp (-z))/2` is literally the odd part of `exp`.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic cosine as even part of exp

anchor.snippet: Hyperbolic cosine: the even part of the exponential function

annotations.note: `Complex.cosh z := (exp z + exp (-z))/2` is the even part of `exp`.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic tangent (exponential)

anchor.snippet: Hyperbolic tangent:

annotations.note: `Real.tanh_eq` gives `tanh x = (exp x - exp(-x))/(exp x + exp(-x))`.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic cotangent (exponential)

anchor.snippet: Hyperbolic cotangent: for x ≠ 0

annotations.note: No coth function or exponential identity for it is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic secant (exponential)

anchor.snippet: Hyperbolic secant:

annotations.note: No sech function is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic cosecant (exponential)

anchor.snippet: Hyperbolic cosecant: for x ≠ 0

annotations.note: No csch function is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sinh and cosh via ODE system

anchor.snippet: The hyperbolic sine and cosine are the solution

annotations.note: Mathlib has `hasDerivAt_sinh`/`hasDerivAt_cosh` (so sinh'=cosh, cosh'=sinh) but does not characterize sinh/cosh as the unique solution of this ODE system.

annotations.label: Sinh and cosh as solutions of f''=f

anchor.snippet: are also the unique solution of the equation

annotations.note: That sinh''=sinh and cosh''=cosh is derivable from `deriv_sinh = cosh`/`deriv_cosh = sinh`, but the uniqueness statement is not formalized.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic functions via complex trig

anchor.snippet: Hyperbolic functions may also be deduced from trigonometric functions with complex arguments

annotations.note: `Complex.sinh_mul_I : sinh (x*I) = sin x * I` and `Complex.cosh_mul_I : cosh (x*I) = cos x` capture this relationship.

annotations.label: Area under cosh equals arc length

anchor.snippet: the area under the curve of the hyperbolic cosine (over a finite interval) is always equal to the arc length corresponding to that interval

annotations.note: No statement about area-under-cosh equalling arc length is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tanh as ODE solution

anchor.snippet: The hyperbolic tangent is the (unique) solution to the differential equation

annotations.note: No ODE-uniqueness characterization of tanh is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Osborn's rule

anchor.snippet: Osborn's rule

annotations.note: Osborn's rule (sign convention for converting trig identities to hyperbolic) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Odd and even functions

anchor.snippet: Odd and even functions:

annotations.note: `sinh_neg : sinh (-x) = -sinh x` and `cosh_neg : cosh (-x) = cosh x` are in Mathlib for both `Real` and `Complex`.

annotations.label: Reciprocals

anchor.snippet: Reciprocals:

annotations.note: Since coth/sech/csch are not defined, their reciprocal identities are not formalized.

annotations.label: Analogue of Euler's formula

anchor.snippet: Analogous to Euler's formula

annotations.note: `Complex.cosh_add_sinh : cosh x + sinh x = exp x` (and `Real` version) formalize the hyperbolic Euler analogue.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic Pythagorean identity

anchor.snippet: Analogous to the Pythagorean trigonometric identity

annotations.note: `Complex.cosh_sq_sub_sinh_sq : cosh x ^ 2 - sinh x ^ 2 = 1` (and Real version).

annotations.label: Sums and differences of arguments

anchor.snippet: particularly

annotations.note: `Complex.sinh_add`, `cosh_add`, `sinh_sub`, `cosh_sub` are all in Mathlib for `Real` and `Complex`.

annotations.label: Half argument formulas

anchor.snippet: where sgn is the sign function

annotations.note: Half-angle formulas for sinh/cosh in terms of the sign function are not formalized for arbitrary real arguments.

annotations.label: Half argument formula for nonzero x

anchor.snippet: If x ≠ 0 then

annotations.note: The half-argument formula for nonzero x is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tangent half argument formula

anchor.snippet: When

annotations.note: No tanh half-argument formula appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Statistical inequality for hyperbolic functions

anchor.snippet: The following inequality is useful in statistics

annotations.note: `Real.cosh_le_exp_half_sq : cosh x ≤ exp (x^2/2)` is the inequality used in statistics (e.g. Hoeffding).

annotations.label: Sinh and cosh equal their second derivatives

anchor.snippet: Each of the functions sinh and cosh is equal to its second derivative

annotations.note: Follows directly from `deriv_sinh = cosh` and `deriv_cosh = sinh`, but no dedicated `sinh'' = sinh` lemma is stated.

annotations.label: Functions equal to own second derivative

anchor.snippet: All functions with this property are linear combinations of sinh and cosh

annotations.note: No general classification of solutions of f''=f as span{sinh,cosh} is formalized.

annotations.label: Integrals via hyperbolic substitution

anchor.snippet: The following integrals can be proved using hyperbolic substitution

annotations.note: Integrals of 1/√(x²±a²), etc. via hyperbolic substitution are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Taylor series of sinh

anchor.snippet: Since the function sinh x is odd , only odd exponents for x occur in its Taylor series

annotations.note: `Real.sinh_eq_tsum`/`Complex.sinh_eq_tsum` give `sinh x = ∑ x^(2n+1)/(2n+1)!`.

annotations.label: Taylor series of cosh

anchor.snippet: Since the function cosh x is even , only even exponents for x occur in its Taylor series

annotations.note: `Real.cosh_eq_tsum`/`Complex.cosh_eq_tsum` give `cosh x = ∑ x^(2n)/(2n)!`.

annotations.label: Sum of sinh and cosh series

anchor.snippet: The sum of the sinh and cosh series is the infinite series expression of the exponential function

annotations.note: `Complex.sinh_add_cosh : sinh x + cosh x = exp x` (plus the tsum identities) gives this; combined with `cosh_eq_tsum`/`sinh_eq_tsum` the series-sum form follows.

annotations.label: Infinite product expansions

anchor.snippet: The following expansions are valid in the whole complex plane

annotations.note: No Weierstrass-product expansions of sinh/cosh appear in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic angle invariance under squeeze mapping

anchor.snippet: The hyperbolic angle is an invariant measure with respect to the squeeze mapping

annotations.note: No formalization of squeeze mappings or hyperbolic-angle invariance.

annotations.label: Gudermannian function relation

anchor.snippet: The Gudermannian function gives a direct relationship between the circular functions and the hyperbolic functions

annotations.note: The Gudermannian function is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Catenary as graph of cosh

anchor.snippet: is the catenary , the curve formed by a uniform flexible chain

annotations.note: The catenary curve is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Exponential decomposition into sinh and cosh

anchor.snippet: The decomposition of the exponential function in its even and odd parts gives the identities

annotations.note: `Complex.cosh_add_sinh : cosh x + sinh x = exp x` and `Complex.cosh_sub_sinh : cosh x - sinh x = exp (-x)` give the decomposition.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic functions for complex arguments

anchor.snippet: we can also extend the definitions of the hyperbolic functions to complex arguments

annotations.note: `Complex.sinh`, `Complex.cosh`, `Complex.tanh` are defined directly on `ℂ`.

annotations.label: sinh z and cosh z are holomorphic

anchor.snippet: The functions sinh z and cosh z are then holomorphic

annotations.note: `Complex.analyticOnNhd_sinh`/`analyticOnNhd_cosh` (and `differentiable_sinh`/`differentiable_cosh`) state holomorphicity.

annotations.label: Periodicity in imaginary component

anchor.snippet: hyperbolic functions are periodic with respect to the imaginary component

annotations.note: `Complex.sinh_periodic`/`cosh_periodic` give period `2π·I`, and `tanh_periodic` gives period `π·I`.

annotations.label: Addition and subtraction formulas

anchor.snippet: Addition and subtraction formulas

annotations.note: `sinh_add`/`cosh_add`/`sinh_sub`/`cosh_sub` exist for both `Real` and `Complex`; no `tanh_add` though.

annotations.label: Product formulas

anchor.snippet: Product formulas

annotations.note: Sum-to-product formulas for hyperbolic functions (sinh+sinh, cosh+cosh, etc.) are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Square formulas

anchor.snippet: Square formulas

annotations.note: `cosh_two_mul`, `sinh_two_mul`, `cosh_sq_sub_sinh_sq`, `sinh_sq`, `cosh_sq` capture the standard square identities.

annotations.label: Inverse hyperbolic functions as logarithms

anchor.snippet: Inverse functions as logarithms

annotations.note: `Real.arsinh`/`Real.arcosh` are defined as logarithm expressions, and `Real.artanh_eq_half_log` gives `artanh x = ½ log((1+x)/(1-x))`.

annotations.label: Derivatives of hyperbolic functions

anchor.snippet: Derivatives

annotations.note: `Real.deriv_sinh`, `Real.deriv_cosh`, plus `hasDerivAt_*` for both `Real` and `Complex` cover sinh/cosh/tanh derivatives.

annotations.label: Standard integrals of hyperbolic functions

anchor.snippet: Standard integrals

annotations.note: No `integral_sinh`/`integral_cosh` antiderivative lemmas are present.

annotations.label: Taylor series convergence on complex plane (sinh)

anchor.snippet: This series is convergent for every complex value of x . Since the function sinh x is odd

annotations.note: `Complex.hasSum_sinh` states convergence of the Taylor series of sinh on all of ℂ.

annotations.label: Taylor series convergence on complex plane (cosh)

anchor.snippet: This series is convergent for every complex value of x . Since the function cosh x is even

annotations.note: `Complex.hasSum_cosh` states convergence of the Taylor series of cosh on all of ℂ.

annotations.label: Circular sector area equals angle when r=√2

anchor.snippet: Since the area of a circular sector with radius r and angle u (in radians) is r 2 u /2 , it will be equal to u when r = √ 2

annotations.note: No circular-sector area formula tying angle to area in this form is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Complex exponential via sinh and cosh

anchor.snippet: this gives

annotations.note: `Complex.cosh_add_sinh : cosh z + sinh z = exp z` is exactly this identity.
